title: Ignition Failure
shortDescription: The burner failed to ignite after multiple attempts.
fullDescription: Error E003 indicates the water heater attempted to ignite the gas burner but did not detect a flame within the ignition window. This is one of the most common Navien error codes.

causes.cause: No gas supply to the unit

symptoms: Unit attempts to start then stops

fixSteps.title: Check gas supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the gas supply valve to the unit is fully open. Check if other gas appliances in the home work.

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Press the power button off, wait 30 seconds, then press on. The unit will attempt to ignite again.

fixSteps.title: Clean the flame rod
fixSteps.instruction: Access the flame rod through the front cover. Gently clean it with fine sandpaper or steel wool to remove buildup.

fixSteps.title: Check the igniter
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the igniter for cracks or damage. It should glow orange when the unit tries to ignite.

whenToCallPro: If the gas valve or control board is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E003 ignition failure error fix flame rod

faq.q: Why does my Navien not ignite?
faq.a: The most common causes are a dirty flame rod, gas supply issue, or failed igniter.

faq.q: How often should I clean the flame rod?
faq.a: Clean the flame rod annually during regular maintenance.

faq.q: Is E003 dangerous?
faq.a: E003 means the unit detected no flame and stopped safely. It is not dangerous.

title: Flame Loss During Operation
shortDescription: The flame was detected at startup but was lost during operation.
fullDescription: Error E012 means the unit successfully ignited but the flame was lost mid-operation. This differs from E003 which is failure to ignite at all.

causes.cause: Wind blowing into exhaust vent causing flame blowout
Dirty or weak flame rod signal

fixSteps.title: Check the exhaust vent
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the exhaust vent termination outside. Ensure the vent cap is installed and wind cannot blow directly into the vent.

fixSteps.title: Clean the flame rod
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the flame rod with fine sandpaper to ensure strong flame detection.

fixSteps.title: Check gas pressure
fixSteps.instruction: A technician should verify the gas supply pressure at the unit meets Navien specifications (WC 3.5-10.5 for natural gas).

fixSteps.title: Inspect condensate drain
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the condensate drain is clear. A blocked drain can cause internal water buildup affecting the burner.

whenToCallPro: If gas pressure issues are suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E012 flame loss error fix vent wind

faq.q: Can wind cause E012?
faq.a: Yes, improper vent termination can allow wind to blow out the flame.

faq.q: How do I check the vent cap?
faq.a: Look at the exterior vent termination. It should have a proper wind-resistant cap.

faq.q: Is E012 different from E003?
faq.a: Yes, E003 is failure to ignite. E012 is flame loss after successful ignition.

title: Overheating Protection Activated
shortDescription: The water temperature inside the unit has exceeded the safe maximum.
fullDescription: Error E016 indicates the heat exchanger temperature has exceeded the overheat safety limit, typically above 95C (203F).

causes.cause: Flow sensor malfunction giving false low flow
Inadequate water flow through the unit

fixSteps.title: Check water flow
fixSteps.instruction: Open multiple hot water taps. If flow is weak, check for closed valves or clogged filter.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet filter
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the cold water supply. Remove and clean the inlet filter screen.

fixSteps.title: Flush the heat exchanger
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a vinegar flush to remove scale. Connect a pump to circulate white vinegar through the unit for 45 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check the flow sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If flushing does not help, the flow sensor may need testing or replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the gas valve is suspected or internal components need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E016 overheating error flush descale fix

faq.q: How often should I flush my Navien?
faq.a: Annually, or every 6 months in hard water areas.

faq.q: Can hard water cause E016?
faq.a: Yes, scale buildup insulates the heat exchanger, causing localized overheating.

faq.q: Is E016 dangerous?
faq.a: The safety system prevents dangerous temperatures. Address the root cause promptly.

title: Exhaust Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The exhaust gas temperature has exceeded the maximum safe limit.
fullDescription: Error E030 indicates the exhaust temperature sensor has detected flue gas temperatures above the safety threshold, indicating combustion or venting issues.

causes.cause: Scale buildup in heat exchanger reducing heat transfer

fixSteps.title: Check the exhaust vent
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the entire exhaust vent run for blockages, bird nests, ice, or damage. Ensure proper slope.

fixSteps.title: Check combustion air supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the combustion air intake is not blocked. Clear any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Flush the heat exchanger
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a descaling flush to improve heat transfer and lower exhaust temperatures.

fixSteps.title: Test exhaust sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the vent is clear and heat exchanger is clean, test the exhaust temperature sensor.

whenToCallPro: If vent modifications are needed.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E030 exhaust temperature high error fix vent

faq.q: What blocks the exhaust vent?
faq.a: Bird nests, ice in winter, wasp nests, and debris are common causes.

faq.q: Can a long vent run cause E030?
faq.a: Yes, exceeding the maximum vent length increases exhaust back pressure and temperature.

faq.q: How hot should exhaust be?
faq.a: Normal exhaust temperature for a condensing unit is 100-140F.

title: Exhaust Blockage or Air Pressure Switch Error
shortDescription: The air pressure switch has detected insufficient combustion airflow.
fullDescription: Error E038 indicates the differential air pressure switch cannot confirm adequate combustion air supply, either from a blocked vent or fan issue.
symptoms: Fan runs but unit does not ignite

fixSteps.title: Check vents
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect both the exhaust and intake vent terminations for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Clear condensate drain
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the condensate drain is flowing freely. A blocked drain can cause water in the exhaust path.

fixSteps.title: Listen for the fan
fixSteps.instruction: When the unit tries to start, listen for the combustion fan. If silent, the fan motor may have failed.

fixSteps.title: Test pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: With the fan running, use a manometer to verify the pressure switch sees adequate differential pressure.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E038 air pressure switch exhaust blockage fix

faq.q: Can wind cause E038?
faq.a: Yes, strong wind can affect the pressure differential and trigger E038.

faq.q: What is the air pressure switch?
faq.a: A safety device that verifies combustion gases are being properly exhausted.

faq.q: How do I clear the condensate drain?
faq.a: Disconnect the drain line and flush with warm water.

title: Outlet Water Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The hot water outlet temperature sensor is reading abnormally.
fullDescription: Error E046 indicates the outlet water temperature sensor (thermistor) has failed, preventing the unit from properly controlling hot water temperature.
symptoms: Scalding hot water or cold water

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Power off for 30 seconds. Restart and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the outlet temperature sensor and ensure the connector is secure.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test the sensor with a multimeter. Compare to Navien specifications.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install a new Navien outlet temperature sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the PCB is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E046 outlet temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Can a faulty sensor cause scalding?
faq.a: Yes, if the sensor reads too low, the unit may overheat the water. Use caution.

faq.q: Where is the outlet sensor?
faq.a: On the hot water outlet pipe inside the unit.

faq.q: Is the sensor expensive?
faq.a: Reasonably priced at $15-$35.

title: Inlet Water Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The cold water inlet temperature sensor is reading abnormally.
fullDescription: Error E047 means the inlet temperature sensor used to calculate the required heat output is malfunctioning.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off 30 seconds, restart.

fixSteps.title: Check connection
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the inlet sensor connector is secure.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance and compare to specs.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install new inlet temperature sensor.

whenToCallPro: If PCB is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E047 inlet temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Why does the inlet sensor matter?
faq.a: The unit calculates needed heat output based on incoming water temperature.

faq.q: Can cold weather affect the sensor?
faq.a: Very cold inlet water is normal, but the sensor itself should not be affected by water temperature.

faq.q: Is this the same as E046?
faq.a: Similar issue but different sensor. E046 is outlet, E047 is inlet.

title: Fan Motor RPM Abnormality
shortDescription: The combustion fan motor is not running at the expected speed.
fullDescription: Error E109 indicates the combustion fan motor speed does not match the commanded RPM, indicating a fan motor, wiring, or control issue.

causes.cause: Dust or debris on fan impeller

symptoms: Fan runs but at wrong speed

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Power off 30 seconds. Restart and listen to fan operation.

fixSteps.title: Clean the fan
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the fan cover and clean dust from the fan impeller and housing.

fixSteps.title: Check fan wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the fan motor wiring for damage or loose connections at the PCB.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor bearings are worn or the motor is not reaching proper RPM, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E109 fan motor RPM error fix

faq.q: How do I know if the fan motor is bad?
faq.a: If it makes grinding noises, vibrates excessively, or does not reach proper speed, it may be failing.

faq.q: Can I clean the fan myself?
faq.a: Yes, basic cleaning is possible by removing the fan access cover.

faq.q: How long does a fan motor last?
faq.a: Typically 8-12 years with proper maintenance.

title: Exhaust Blockage Detection
shortDescription: The exhaust system has detected a blockage preventing proper venting.
fullDescription: Error E110 is a specific exhaust blockage detection separate from E038, indicating the unit has confirmed an exhaust restriction through multiple sensor readings.

causes.cause: Bird nest or debris in exhaust vent

symptoms: Error more common in cold weather

fixSteps.title: Inspect exhaust termination
fixSteps.instruction: Go outside and check the exhaust vent exit for blockages, ice, nests, or debris.

fixSteps.title: Check intake vent
fixSteps.instruction: Also inspect the air intake vent for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Clear any ice
fixSteps.instruction: In winter, carefully remove ice from the vent termination. Consider adding a vent extension.

fixSteps.title: Professional vent inspection
fixSteps.instruction: If no visible blockage, a technician should inspect the entire vent run.

whenToCallPro: If the vent run needs modification.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E110 exhaust blockage vent fix

faq.q: Can ice block the vent?
faq.a: Yes, condensation from the exhaust can freeze at the vent termination in cold weather.

faq.q: How do I prevent ice blockage?
faq.a: Install a vent extension that points downward to prevent moisture accumulation.

faq.q: Is exhaust smell dangerous?
faq.a: Exhaust fumes contain carbon monoxide. If you smell exhaust, ventilate the area and call a technician immediately.

title: Flow Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water flow sensor is not providing accurate readings.
fullDescription: Error E302 indicates the flow sensor that measures water volume through the unit is malfunctioning, preventing proper burner modulation.

causes.cause: Flow sensor magnet or hall sensor failed
Scale buildup on flow sensor impeller

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet filter
fixSteps.instruction: Close the cold water valve. Remove and clean the inlet filter screen.

fixSteps.title: Flush the flow sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a vinegar flush focused on clearing the flow sensor area.

fixSteps.title: Check flow sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the flow sensor connector is secure at the PCB.

fixSteps.title: Replace flow sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not fix E302, install a new flow sensor assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the PCB flow circuit is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E302 flow sensor error fix flush

faq.q: What does the flow sensor do?
faq.a: It measures water flow rate so the unit can modulate the burner for consistent temperature.

faq.q: Can hard water damage the flow sensor?
faq.a: Yes, scale buildup can jam the impeller or block the sensor.

faq.q: How do I prevent flow sensor issues?
faq.a: Annual flushing and an inlet filter screen help prevent debris and scale problems.

title: Recirculation Pump Error
shortDescription: The built-in recirculation pump has a fault or is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: Error E407 indicates the internal recirculation pump (on models with built-in recirc) has failed or cannot maintain expected performance.

fixSteps.title: Purge air from pump
fixSteps.instruction: Open the air purge valve on the recirculation pump to bleed any trapped air.

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Power off for 30 seconds and restart.

fixSteps.title: Check pump operation
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the recirc pump when it should be running. Feel for vibration at the pump housing.

fixSteps.title: Clean or replace pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump is seized, it may need cleaning or replacement of the pump cartridge.

whenToCallPro: If the pump motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E407 recirculation pump error fix

faq.q: What is the recirculation pump?
faq.a: It keeps hot water circulating so you get instant hot water at fixtures far from the unit.

faq.q: Can air cause E407?
faq.a: Yes, air in the pump is the most common and easiest-to-fix cause.

faq.q: How do I bleed air from the pump?
faq.a: Open the small purge valve on the pump while the system is running.

title: Abnormal PCB Operation
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal operational error.
fullDescription: Error E439 indicates the main PCB has detected an abnormality in its own operation, including communication bus errors or component failures.

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Power off for 2 minutes. A temporary glitch may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the PCB area for signs of water leaks or condensation.

fixSteps.title: Install surge protection
fixSteps.instruction: Use a surge protector on the power supply to prevent future PCB damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace the PCB
fixSteps.instruction: If E439 persists, the PCB needs professional replacement.

whenToCallPro: If E439 persists after reset.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E439 PCB control board error fix

faq.q: Is the PCB expensive?
faq.a: Navien PCBs typically cost $150-$350 for the part.

faq.q: Can I replace the PCB myself?
faq.a: Navien recommends professional installation to ensure proper configuration.

faq.q: Does warranty cover PCB failure?
faq.a: If within the warranty period and not caused by a power surge, usually yes.

title: Mixing Valve Error
shortDescription: The thermostatic mixing valve is not functioning correctly (applicable models).
fullDescription: Error E515 indicates the built-in mixing valve is not regulating the output temperature properly.
symptoms: Output temperature not matching set point

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Power off 30 seconds and restart.

fixSteps.title: Flush the system
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a vinegar flush to remove scale from the mixing valve.

fixSteps.title: Check valve motor
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the mixing valve motor actuating. If silent, the motor may be failed.

fixSteps.title: Replace mixing valve
fixSteps.instruction: If flushing does not help, replace the mixing valve assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the valve motor or PCB control circuit has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E515 mixing valve error fix flush

faq.q: What is the mixing valve?
faq.a: It blends hot and cold water to deliver a precise output temperature.

faq.q: Can hard water damage the mixing valve?
faq.a: Yes, scale buildup can prevent the valve from operating smoothly.

faq.q: Is E515 only on certain models?
faq.a: E515 appears on models with a built-in thermostatic mixing valve.

title: Gas Valve Relay Error
shortDescription: The gas valve relay on the PCB is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error E421 indicates the PCB relay that controls the gas valve is stuck open or closed, or the gas valve itself is not responding to commands.

causes.cause: Wiring fault between PCB and gas valve

symptoms: Gas smell after error (if valve stuck open)

fixSteps.title: Turn off gas immediately
fixSteps.instruction: If you smell gas, turn off the gas supply valve to the unit immediately.

fixSteps.title: Reset the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Power off for 2 minutes. If E421 returns, do not continue using the unit.

fixSteps.title: Check gas valve wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring between the PCB and gas valve for damage.

fixSteps.title: Professional repair required
fixSteps.instruction: E421 requires professional diagnosis of the PCB relay and gas valve.

whenToCallPro: Always for E421. Gas valve issues require professional repair.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E421 gas valve relay error fix

faq.q: Is E421 dangerous?
faq.a: If you smell gas, it could be serious. Turn off the gas supply and call a professional immediately.

faq.q: Can I fix the gas valve myself?
faq.a: No. Gas valve and relay repairs should only be done by qualified technicians.

faq.q: Does E421 mean I need a new PCB?
faq.a: Often yes, if the relay on the PCB has failed. Sometimes just the gas valve needs replacement.

title: Abnormal Combustion
shortDescription: The unit has detected abnormal combustion conditions.
fullDescription: Error E480 indicates the combustion analysis or flame characteristics are abnormal, suggesting incomplete combustion or air-fuel ratio issues.

causes.cause: Gas type mismatch (NG vs LP conversion issue)

fixSteps.title: Check air supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the combustion air intake is not blocked or restricted.

fixSteps.title: Verify gas type
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the unit is configured for the correct gas type (natural gas or propane).

fixSteps.title: Clean the burner
fixSteps.instruction: A technician should inspect and clean the burner assembly.

fixSteps.title: Check gas pressure
fixSteps.instruction: A technician should verify inlet and manifold gas pressures.

whenToCallPro: If combustion analysis or gas pressure adjustment is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E480 abnormal combustion error fix

faq.q: Is E480 a carbon monoxide risk?
faq.a: Abnormal combustion can produce elevated CO. Ensure proper ventilation and call a technician.

faq.q: What color should the flame be?
faq.a: A properly burning gas flame should be blue with small tips of yellow.

faq.q: Can altitude affect combustion?
faq.a: Yes, high altitude installations may require gas pressure adjustments.

title: Bypass Valve Error
shortDescription: The internal bypass valve is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: Error E517 indicates the bypass servo valve that controls minimum flow and temperature is malfunctioning.
symptoms: Unit activates at lower flow than expected

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off 30 seconds and restart.

fixSteps.title: Flush the system
fixSteps.instruction: Vinegar flush to remove scale from the bypass valve.

fixSteps.title: Check valve motor
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the bypass valve actuating during operation.

fixSteps.title: Replace bypass valve
fixSteps.instruction: Install a replacement bypass valve assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the valve motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E517 bypass valve error fix flush

faq.q: What does the bypass valve do?
faq.a: It controls water flow and helps maintain minimum activation flow rates.

faq.q: Can flushing fix E517?
faq.a: Yes, if scale is the cause. Flushing resolves many E517 errors.

faq.q: How often should I flush?
faq.a: Annually, or every 6 months with hard water.

title: Cascade Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication failure between multiple linked Navien units in a cascade system.
fullDescription: Error E780 occurs in cascade installations where multiple Navien units are linked together and cannot communicate with each other.
symptoms: E780 on one or more units
Units fire individually but not together
Error after adding a unit to cascade

fixSteps.title: Reset all units
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off all units in the cascade for 2 minutes. Restart in sequence.

fixSteps.title: Check cascade wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the cascade communication cable between all units for damage or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Verify address settings
fixSteps.instruction: Each unit must have a unique cascade address. Verify via the DIP switches or control panel.

fixSteps.title: Professional cascade setup
fixSteps.instruction: Complex cascade issues may require Navien technical support for configuration.

whenToCallPro: If cascade configuration is complex.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E780 cascade communication error fix

faq.q: What is a cascade system?
faq.a: Multiple Navien units linked together for higher combined output.

faq.q: How many units can be cascaded?
faq.a: Up to 16 units depending on the model and controller.

faq.q: Does each unit need a unique address?
faq.a: Yes, each unit in the cascade must have a unique address.

title: DHW Heat Exchanger Thermistor Fault
shortDescription: The domestic hot water heat exchanger temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: Error E045 indicates the DHW heat exchanger thermistor is reading abnormally, preventing proper temperature regulation.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off 30 seconds and restart.

fixSteps.title: Flush the system
fixSteps.instruction: Vinegar flush to remove scale from the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Check sensor resistance with multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install matching replacement sensor.

whenToCallPro: If PCB is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E045 DHW thermistor fix

faq.q: What is the DHW thermistor?
faq.a: It monitors the temperature of the domestic hot water heat exchanger.

faq.q: Can scale affect this sensor?
faq.a: Yes, scale buildup can insulate the sensor causing inaccurate readings.

faq.q: How often should I flush?
faq.a: Annually, or every 6 months with hard water.

title: 3-Way Valve Error
shortDescription: The 3-way diverter valve for space heating is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error E205 indicates the 3-way valve that switches between domestic hot water and space heating modes is stuck or not responding to commands.
symptoms: No switching between DHW and heating

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off 30 seconds and restart.

fixSteps.title: Flush the system
fixSteps.instruction: Flush to remove scale from the valve.

fixSteps.title: Check valve motor
fixSteps.instruction: Listen for the valve motor when switching modes.

fixSteps.title: Replace the valve
fixSteps.instruction: Install new 3-way valve assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the valve motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E205 3-way valve error fix

faq.q: What is the 3-way valve?
faq.a: It diverts water flow between the domestic hot water circuit and the space heating circuit.

faq.q: Does E205 only affect combo units?
faq.a: Yes, E205 is specific to Navien combi-boiler models.

faq.q: Can flushing fix E205?
faq.a: Yes, if scale is preventing the valve from moving.

title: Water Pressure Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water pressure sensor is reading an abnormal value.
fullDescription: Error E351 indicates the system water pressure sensor is out of range, indicating either very low or very high system pressure.

fixSteps.title: Check system pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the system pressure gauge. Normal is 15-30 PSI.

fixSteps.title: Add water to system
fixSteps.instruction: If pressure is low, use the fill valve to add water until pressure reaches 15-20 PSI.

fixSteps.title: Check expansion tank
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Schrader valve on the expansion tank. If water comes out, the tank is waterlogged and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check for leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the entire system for water leaks causing pressure loss.

whenToCallPro: If there is a system leak or the expansion tank needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Navien E351 water pressure sensor error fix

faq.q: What should system pressure be?
faq.a: Normal operating pressure is 15-30 PSI.

faq.q: Why does my system lose pressure?
faq.a: Common causes are leaks, a waterlogged expansion tank, or a leaking pressure relief valve.

faq.q: Can I add pressure myself?
faq.a: Yes, using the fill valve to slowly add water until the gauge reads the correct pressure.
